title: Software Engineer – Full Stack - Internal AI Tools Team
company_focus: Defense, national security, and space technology company
description:
Voyager is an innovative defense, national security and
space technology company committed to advancing and
delivering transformative, mission-critical solutions. We
tackle the most complex challenges to unlock new frontiers
for human progress, fortify national security, and protect
critical assets to lead in the race for technological and
operational superiority from ground to space.

Forge the Future: Join Voyager Technologies

The future belongs to those who build it. At Voyager
Technologies, we’re building technologies that protect
lives, expand frontiers and prepare us for what’s next. And
we’re doing that with people who are wired to solve, build,
adapt and lead. These roles are not for the faint of heart. 

You’ll help lay the foundation for humanity's future. Join a
culture where innovation thrives, curiosity is rewarded, and
impact is real. We’re a company of doers, thinkers and
builders, united by purpose and grounded in reality.

If you want to put your skills to work where the stakes are
real and the mission is bigger than any one person, forge
the future with Voyager.

____________________________________________________________
________________________

Job Summary:

We are seeking a Full Stack Software Engineer to build the
frontend and backend of the Agentic Computational
Engineering platform. In this role, you will create high-
performance full stack web applications that allow engineers
to steer AI agents, visualize complex physics and CAD data,
and collaborate with AI agents in real time.

You will work across the stack, with an emphasis on
performance, 3D visualization, and intuitive UX for
technical users. This role is ideal for someone who loves
building tools for other engineers and who wants to make the
reasoning and actions of AI systems transparent.

You will be joining the Agentic Computational Engineering
(ACE) team, a specialized group within our Advanced
Technology Development organization. ACE is responsible for
building Voyager’s Generative Engine – the AI-native
platform that compresses complex hardware development cycles
from years to days. We design agentic AI systems that pair
deeply with physics simulation, test data, and modern
manufacturing so that Design for Manufacturing (DfM), Design
for Assembly (DfA), and Design for Test (DfT) are built into
the very first line of code and the very first sketch of a
design.

Responsibilities:

- Design and build web applications that serve as mission
  control for ACE, with a focus on responsiveness and
  usability for expert users.
- Implement high-fidelity 2D/3D visualizations (e.g., CAD
  models, PCB layouts, CFD fields) using technologies such
  as Three.js, WebGL, or WebGPU.
- Create features that expose agent reasoning, tool usage, and
  design history in transparent and intuitive ways for
  engineers.
- Develop real-time, collaborative editing and review
  workflows (e.g., via WebSockets, CRDTs) for human-AI co-
  design.
- Collaborate with backend, platform, and domain team members
  to design APIs and data contracts that power the UI.
- Manage agentic teams of LLMs to scaffold components,
  generate documentation, and accelerate development.

Required Qualifications:

- An ability to define design decisions and build rapidly in a
  scalable way.
- 1–5 years of experience as a full-stack or frontend-focused
  software engineer (exceptional new graduates encouraged to
  apply with a strong portfolio).
- Experience with modern web frameworks such as React and
  Next.js, and with state management for complex
  applications.
- Strong understanding of JavaScript/TypeScript, HTTP APIs,
  and browser performance optimization.
- Exposure to 3D graphics or data visualization on the web
  (WebGL/WebGPU/Three.js or similar) through professional
  work, coursework, or personal projects.
- Demonstrated habit of using AI tools to accelerate
  implementation, styling, and documentation.

Preferred Qualifications:

- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Physics,
  Mathematics or a related field; or equivalent experience.
- Experience building software tools for engineers,
  scientists, or technical operations teams.
- Portfolio of creative coding, generative art, or game
  development projects.
- Familiarity with design systems and UX best practices for
  technical users.
- Deep passion for automation.

Voyager offers a comprehensive, total compensation package,
which includes competitive salary, a discretionary annual
bonus plan, paid time off (PTO), a comprehensive health
benefit package, retirement savings, wellness program, and
various other benefits. When you join our team, you’re not
just an employee; you become part of a dynamic community
dedicated to innovation and excellence.

To conform to U.S. Government space technology export
regulations, including the International Traffic in Arms
Regulations (ITAR) you must be a U.S. citizen, lawful
permanent resident of the U.S., protected individual as
defined by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3), or eligible to obtain the
required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.

Voyager is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Employment
decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion,
national or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender
identity or expression, age, disability, protected veteran
status or other characteristics protected by law. 

Minority/Female/Disabled/Veteran

The statements contained in this job description are
intended to describe the general content and requirements
for performance of this job. It is not intended to be an
exhaustive list of all job duties, responsibilities, and
requirements. This job description is not an employment
agreement or contract. Management has the exclusive right to
alter the scope of work within the framework of this job
description at any time without prior notice.

responsibilities: Design and build web applications that serve as mission control for ACE, with focus on responsiveness and usability for expert users
Implement high-fidelity 2D/3D visualizations of CAD models, PCB layouts, and CFD fields using Three.js, WebGL, or WebGPU
Create features that expose agent reasoning, tool usage, and design history in transparent and intuitive ways for engineers
Develop real-time collaborative editing and review workflows using WebSockets or CRDTs for human-AI co-design
Collaborate with backend, platform, and domain team members to design APIs and data contracts that power the UI

use_cases: Agentic AI platform for computational engineering that compresses hardware development cycles from years to days
AI-native design system integrating Design for Manufacturing, Design for Assembly, and Design for Test into initial design stages
Real-time collaboration between human engineers and AI agents for hardware and CAD design workflows
Visualization and control interface for steering AI agents working on physics simulations and hardware development
Transparent AI agent reasoning and action tracking for engineering decision-making and review processes
ai_classification_reasoning:
This is a full-stack web engineering role focused on
building UI/frontend tools for AI agents, not building or
deploying AI models directly. The engineer creates
visualization interfaces, dashboards, and collaboration
features to interact with AI systems, but does not train,
fine-tune, or implement the underlying models or agents.
